key_insight: Surface reconstruction stability on GaN(0001)-(6×6) can be predicted by tiling decomposition multiplicity: the configuration with the most compatible tilings is usually the most stable.
engineering_relevance: This turns an intractable DFT-guided configuration search into an exhaustive discrete enumeration strategy, sharply reducing the number of candidates that need first-principles evaluation.
method: Model the EC-satisfying GaN(0001)-(6×6) surface as a rhombus tiling problem.
Enumerate all tilings and all compatible adatom arrangements to build the full configuration catalog.
Rank configurations by tiling decomposition multiplicity and validate with ML potential screening plus DFT
evidence_signal: The authors enumerated 416,683 fixed-stoichiometry configurations, reduced them to 24 DFT-evaluated candidates, and validated the screening with an ML interatomic potential trained/checked against 710 DFT structures.
watch_item: The rule is not a universal exact law: one symmetry class shows a near-degeneracy where the most stable and highest-multiplicity configurations differ by only 8.5 meV, so final ranking still requires DFT.
paper_title: Tiling decomposition multiplicity predicts stability of GaN(0001) surface reconstructions
